text.title: Feature Launch Checklist Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Feature Launch Checklist. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: 20 items run every launch; skipped items carry a written reason on the artefact.
rule.rationale: Silent skip is the dominant launch-checklist failure.

rule.statement: One named owner accountable for launch-day execution; not 'marketing' or 'team'.
rule.rationale: Plural launch ownership ships incomplete launches.

rule.statement: Launch date is fixed and copied to every channel asset. Slips trigger a re-cycle of all assets.
rule.rationale: Floating dates desync assets across channels.

rule.statement: Hit a minimum of 3 channels (owned, earned, paid or community). Single-channel launches under-reach.
rule.rationale: Single-channel launch reaches <10% of addressable audience.

rule.statement: Success metric (signups, demos, revenue) is declared before launch, with a 30-day check date.
rule.rationale: Post-hoc metric selection allows narrative manipulation.

rule.statement: Support docs / FAQ / canned replies live before launch day.
rule.rationale: Day-1 support load tanks NPS without enablement.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-feature-launch-checklist.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
